title: What would George Washington say? It's a busy year for people who portray him
description: America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.

title: Trump tries out midterms message that focuses on ‘communists’
description: Trump warned an audience of religious conservatives that communism is spreading in the U.S., a new theme for his midterm campaign.

title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
description: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
description: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

title: Trump's demands for loyalty are causing friction
description: President Trump has demanded loyalty from his supporters and allies, causing friction within the Republican party and abroad.

title: State Department spokesman on Venezuela response, Iran negotiations
description: A conversation with Thomas "Tommy" Pigott, spokesperson for the U.S. State Department.

title: She posted about ICE. Five months later, DHS agents told her to take her post down
description: In January, Paigelynne Gonyea posted about the immigration surge in Minneapolis. This week, she was visited by ICE officials who claimed one of her posts had doxxed a federal agent.

title: John Bolton, former Trump national security adviser, pleads guilty in classified documents case
description: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."

title: Ex-NOAA employees re-create a valuable climate data site shut down by Trump
description: Former NOAA staffers have launched a new website that provides climate information. It replaces a government site that was shut down when the Trump administration took office.

title: Organizers say the Great American State Fair is for everyone. Some disagree
description: The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.

title: Problem Solvers Caucus co-chairs say they'll make sure housing bill becomes law
description: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., tell NPR's Michel Martin they want to force into law a housing bill President Trump has refused to sign.

title: Inside Trump’s plans for retribution and other lesser-known allegations in ‘Regime Change’
description: A new book from two Washington reporters reveals what took place inside the White House after President Donald Trump’s return to power.

title: Rubio’s new job? Selling Trump’s Iran deal to a worried Gulf.
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Arab Gulf leaders to reassure them that U.S. isn’t cutting them out of negotiations with Tehran.

title: Migrants not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border, Supreme Court rules
description: The 6-3 decision clears the way for the Trump administration to resume allowing federal agents at the border to turn back asylum seekers before they enter.

title: Democratic socialists cemented power in New York. Next, the rest of the country?
description: The Democratic Socialists of America won with support outside their usual base. Winning outside New York may be a different challenge.
